summary: Role-based Channel Connections page view testing complete. Super admin sees full technical view with HotelRunner/Exely provider cards, credentials, and management buttons. Hotel staff (front_desk) sees simplified view with only connected OTA channel names - no provider names or technical details visible.

Super Admin sees HotelRunner provider card with HR ID 373816343 - PASSED

Front Desk does NOT see Test/Disconnect buttons - PASSED

critical_code_review_comments: ChannelConnections.jsx: Role-based view correctly implemented using isSuperAdmin = user?.role === 'super_admin' check at line 31
ChannelConnections.jsx: Hotel user view (non-superadmin) shows only OTA channel names without revealing provider names (HotelRunner/Exely)
ChannelConnections.jsx: Super admin view shows full technical details including HR ID, Otel Kodu, credentials, and management buttons
Note: Channel names like 'HotelRunner Ad Network' and 'HotelRunner B2B Network' are legitimate OTA channel names (not provider names) and correctly appear in hotel user view
seed_data_creation: None required - using existing demo tenant data
context_for_next_testing_agent: Channel Connections page has role-based views. Super admin (role: super_admin) sees full technical view at /channel-connections with provider cards, credentials, and management buttons. Hotel staff (any non-super_admin role like front_desk) sees simplified view showing only connected OTA channel names without provider details. The 'HotelRunner Ad Network' and 'HotelRunner B2B Network' appearing in channel list are legitimate OTA channel names, not the provider name.

super_admin_view.hotelrunner_card: VISIBLE - Shows HR ID, environment, room mappings, connected channels
super_admin_view.exely_card: VISIBLE - Shows Otel Kodu, currency, room mappings

front_desk_view.channel_grid: VISIBLE - 132 channel cards with green checkmarks
